title: gaultheria tetracme
intro: Gaultheria tetracme, also known as Mountain Tea or Mountain Cranberry, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, native to eastern Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Gaultheria tetracme include its ability to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and soothe digestive issues. Gaultheria tetracme can be prepared as a herbal tea or used in tincture form, with the dried leaves and flowers often being used in traditional medicine.
